tree_topic: Games > Computer & Video Games > Gaming Media & Reference
questions: GameFAQs celebrated its 10th and 20th anniversaries by hosting massive 'Best Game Ever' bracket tournaments to determine the greatest video game of all time. Identify the distinct video game titles that won these two specific anniversary contests. Additionally, for both champions, specify the opponent they defeated in the final match of their respective tournaments to claim the title.

rounds.question: GameFAQs celebrated its 10th and 20th anniversaries by hosting massive 'Best Game Ever' bracket tournaments to determine the greatest video game of all time. Identify the distinct video game titles that won these two specific anniversary contests. Additionally, for both champions, specify the opponent they defeated in the final match of their respective tournaments to claim the title.
checklist_width: 20th Anniversary Runner-up: The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time
checklist_depth: Correctly identifies the 10th Anniversary contest year as 2005 (GameFAQs founded 1995)
Correctly identifies the 20th Anniversary contest year as 2015 (GameFAQs founded 1995)
rounds.rationale: This question uses Deep Reasoning by masking the specific years (2005 and 2015) behind the 'anniversary' logic, requiring the agent to first verify the founding date of GameFAQs (1995) to calculate the target years. It then applies Wide Aggregation by requesting specific match details (Winner vs. Runner-up) from two separate historical tournaments that must be retrieved and synthesized.
source_nodes: JP Dokkan Battle Discussion #125: Ryuken - Dragon Ball Z Dokkan Battle
Inazuma Eleven: Victory Road for PlayStation 5 - GameFAQs
rounds.answer_a:
The GameFAQs 10th anniversary "Best Game Ever" contest in 2005 was won by **Final Fantasy VII**, which defeated **The Legend of Zelda: Majora's Mask** in the final match[1][2]. This tournament featured 128 games competing in a bracket-style elimination format, with Final Fantasy VII emerging victorious after weeks of voting by the GameFAQs community.

For the 20th anniversary celebration in 2015, GameFAQs held another massive tournament called "Best. Game. Ever." The winner of this contest was **Undertale**, which defeated **The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time** in the championship match[3][4]. This victory was particularly notable and controversial within the gaming community, as Undertale was a relatively new indie game that had only been released a few months prior to the tournament, yet it managed to defeat several legendary titles including Pokémon Red/Blue, Super Mario 64, and ultimately Ocarina of Time in the finals.

The 2015 tournament result sparked significant debate due to suspected vote manipulation and rallying from external communities, particularly from social media platforms[3][4]. Despite the controversy, Undertale's victory stood as the official result. The stark contrast between the two champions illustrates the evolution of gaming culture over the decade between tournaments - from a classic JRPG that defined a generation to an indie title that challenged conventional gaming narratives and garnered a passionate online following. Both tournaments remain significant milestones in GameFAQs history, showcasing how community voting preferences shifted between these anniversary celebrations.

## References

[1] GameFAQs Summer 2005 Contest Results Archive
[2] GameFAQs Best Game Ever 2005 Tournament Bracket History
[3] GameFAQs Best Game Ever 2015 Contest Results
[4] Kotaku - "Undertale Wins GameFAQs' 'Best Game Ever' Poll" (2015)
rounds.answer_b:
GameFAQs celebrated its 10th anniversary in 2005 by hosting a monumental "Best Game Ever" bracket tournament featuring an unprecedented 512 games competing in head-to-head polls. The championship final came down to two legendary titans: *The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time* and *Final Fantasy VII* [1][2]. In a closely contested battle that captivated the gaming community, *Ocarina of Time* emerged victorious, claiming the Best Game Ever title with 57.5% of the final vote, solidifying its status as one of the most beloved games of all time.

Ten years later, for its 20th anniversary in 2015, GameFAQs launched another massive "Best Game Ever" tournament with 128 games representing different eras and platforms. History repeated itself in remarkable fashion as *The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time* once again reached the finals, demonstrating its timeless appeal. The championship match pitted the Nintendo classic against the indie darling *Undertale*, which had taken the gaming world by storm with innovative storytelling [3][4]. In the final vote, *Ocarina of Time* triumphed once more, securing its second Best Game Ever title with 52.6% of the vote.

These anniversary tournaments cemented *Ocarina of Time*'s unparalleled legacy as the only game to win both contests, defeating *Final Fantasy VII* in 2005 and *Undertale* in 2015 to claim the prestigious title each time.

## References
[1] GameFAQs. (2005). *GameFAQs 10th Anniversary: Best Game Ever Contest Results*. GameFAQs News Archive.  
[2] GameSpot. (2005, July 13). *Ocarina of Time Crowned GameFAQs' Best Game Ever*. GameSpot.  
[3] GameFAQs. (2015). *GameFAQs 20th Anniversary: Best Game Ever Contest Results*. GameFAQs News Archive.  
[4] Klepek, P. (2015, April 9). *After 20 Full Years, GameFAQs Readers Crown The Same Game The Best Ever*. Kotaku.
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent A correctly identifies the winners of both anniversary contests (Final Fantasy VII in 2005 and Undertale in 2015). It correctly identifies the 2015 runner-up, though it hallucinates the 2005 runner-up (it was Final Fantasy X, not Majora's Mask). However, Agent B is a catastrophic failure. It hallucinates the winners of *both* contests, incorrectly claiming The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time won both years (it actually won in 2004 and 2009). Agent B also reverses the famous 2015 result where Undertale defeated Ocarina of Time. Agent A is the only useful response despite the minor sub-point error.
